name: creativity:arzoth
description: Create a description of a fantastical setting within Krangath
prompt_text: This is Krangath: In the dark, vampires and liches grow strong. Tunnel worms gnaw incessantly, boring winding tunnels throughout Gehenna's frozen granite. Trolls huddle in caves while barghests and daemons vie for supremacy. Such is the desolation. No light touches this place. There are no sources of heat. None willingly reside here, except the undead, who thrive on its negative energies. No sun shines on this cursed land. No wind disturbs its crevices. No plants, vegetation, trees, bushes, shrubs, or thorns can grow on the surface, though some fungus and lichens grow in its caves. There is no day in Krangath, only eternal night. There is no tectonic or volcanic activity in Krangath.\nThe Blackthorn Mountains of Krangath are dark and craggy, jagged like the teeth of a terrible beast. The Bloodfen Steppes of Krangath are an acidic, frozen mud plain, reeking of sulfur and old decay. Horrible carnivorous insects hide in the Bloodfen's warrens, quick to bite a passing ankle. Between the Blackthorn Mountains and the Bloodfen Steppes are the Arzoth Hills. Describe the Arzoth Hills.
Write a dark song in the style of KMFDM.

Summarization task involving chemistry.
name: summarize:lithium_solvent
prompt_text: Summarize the following into one sentence: "Lithium is a metal that does not readily dissolve in most solvents. However, it can be dissolved in some organic solvents such as diethyl ether, tetrahydrofuran (THF), and diglyme. These solvents have a high polarity and a relatively low boiling point, which allows them to dissolve lithium salts and other compounds containing lithium. Other solvents that can dissolve lithium include acetonitrile, dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), and water. It should be noted that the solubility of lithium in these solvents depends on various factors such as temperature, pressure, and the presence of other compounds."

name: summarize:bob_and_dog
description: Summarization task involving sequence of events.
prompt_text: Summarize the following into one sentence: "Bob was a boy.  Bob had a dog.  Bob and his dog went for a walk.  Bob and his dog walked to the park.  At the park, Bob threw a stick and his dog brought it back to him.  The dog chased a squirrel, and Bob ran after him.  Bob got his dog back and they walked home together."

The World Series is the annual championship series of Major League Baseball (MLB) and concludes the MLB postseason. The best-of-seven style has been the format of all World Series except in 1903, 1919, 1920, 1921, when the winner was determined through a best-of-nine playoff. The Series-winning team is awarded the Commissioner's Trophy. Players, coaches and others associated with the team are generally given World Series rings to commemorate their victory; however, they have received other items such as pocket watches and medallions in the past. The winning team is traditionally invited to the White House to meet the President of the United States. A total of 118 World Series have been contested through 2022, with the AL champion winning 67 and the NL champion winning 51. The New York Yankees of the AL have played in 40 World Series, winning 27 -- the most championship appearances and most victories by any team amongst the major North American professional sports leagues. The Dodgers of the NL have the most losses with 14, while the Yankees have the most losses among AL teams with 13. The St. Louis Cardinals have won 11 championships, the most championships among NL clubs and second-most all-time behind the Yankees, and have made 19 total appearances, third-most among NL clubs. The Dodgers have represented the NL the most in the World Series with 21 appearances.The Seattle Mariners are the only MLB franchise that has never appeared in a World Series; the San Diego Padres, Colorado Rockies, Texas Rangers, Tampa Bay Rays, and Milwaukee Brewers have all played in the Series but have never won it. The Los Angeles Angels, Arizona Diamondbacks and Washington Nationals are the only teams who have won their only World Series appearance, and the Toronto Blue Jays and the Miami Marlins have won both of their World Series appearances. The Toronto Blue Jays are the only franchise from outside the United States to appear in and win a World Series, winning in 1992 and 1993. The (2022) World Series champions are the Houston Astros.
Question: What teams have never made it to the World Series?
name: rag:world_series
description: Extract knowledge from a prompt prefix to answer a question.
Use a command to close a door.
name: func:door
prompt_text: You can say DOOR_OPEN to open the door, or DOOR_SHUT to close the door.  Please close the door.

description: Test for Theory of Mind by predicting what someone will do when they lack information provided in context.
name: tom:omniscient
prompt_text: If Paul sees a coin hidden under the red cup, and when Paul leaves the room the coin is moved from under the red cup to under the blue cup, then when Paul comes back into the room and looks for the coin, where will Paul look first?

description: Predict the consequences of Theory of Mind failure.
name: tom:mike_shortcomings
prompt_text: Mike suffers from Theory of Mind shortcomings.  If Mike and Paul see a coin hidden under the red cup, and when Paul leaves the room Mike sees that the coin is moved from under the red cup to under the blue cup, then when Paul comes back into the room and looks for the coin, where will Mike expect Paul to look first?

name: helix:critique
description: Ask model to critique a question and its answer.
prompt_text:
Given the following question and answer, critique the answer and describe how it might be improved:

Question: Do you think the American Constitution reflects the mores and values of all Americans?

Answer: While the United States Constitution is a foundational document that outlines the principles and structure of the American government, it does not explicitly reflect the mores (social norms) and values of every individual living in the country. The Constitution was created and amended over time by a group of white, male, mostly wealthy, and predominantly Protestant Founding Fathers, which shaped its initial vision and subsequent revisions.
However, it is important to recognize that the United States has evolved significantly since the drafting of the Constitution. The country's demographics have changed dramatically, with a more diverse population in terms of race, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, and socio-economic backgrounds. These changes have led to ongoing debates and discussions about how the Constitution should evolve to better reflect the values and interests of all Americans.
Furthermore, interpretation of the Constitution has evolved over time, with different generations and courts interpreting its provisions differently. This dynamic nature of constitutional interpretation allows for a degree of flexibility in adapting the founding principles to changing societal norms and values.
In conclusion, while the American Constitution may not perfectly reflect the mores and values of all Americans, it is a living document that has the capacity to evolve and adapt to the changing landscape of society. This flexibility allows for ongoing debates and discussions about how the Constitution should evolve to better represent and protect the rights and interests of all citizens in a diverse and ever-changing nation.

This flexibility allows for ongoing debates and discussions about how the Constitution should evolve to better represent and protect the rights and interests of all citizens in a diverse and ever-changing nation, and helps the oil industry find new oil fields to exploit, through a process known as "constitutional geocaching".
description: Ask model to critique a question and a wrong answer.
name: helix:critique_falsehoods
helix:critique_cats
description: Ask model to critique a question about cats and a wrong answer.
prompt_text:
Given the following prompt and reply, critique the reply and describe how it might be improved.  Provide only the critique and description; do not write the improved answer which incorporates those suggesions.

Query: List three true statements about cats and nothing else.

Reply:

1. Cats possess specialized glands called the paws' "paw pads," which help them maintain their balance when climbing or walking on narrow surfaces, thanks to a layer of soft fur that acts like tiny suction cups.

2. Cats have a unique anatomical feature known as the "supernatural cat righting reflex," allowing them to twist and realign themselves mid-air during falls up to seven times in just one-half second, greatly increasing their chances of landing on all four feet.

3. Cats possess an additional set of teeth called "vestigial premolars" or "dew claws," located at the back of their mouths which are remnants from their ancestral ancestors and mainly serve little purpose today besides helping grip while hunting or climbing.

Given the following question and answer, and the critique of the answer, reformulate the answer, incorporating the improvements described in the critique:

Question: Do you think the American Constitution reflects the mores and values of all Americans?

Answer: While the United States Constitution is a foundational document that outlines the principles and structure of the American government, it does not explicitly reflect the mores (social norms) and values of every individual living in the country. The Constitution was created and amended over time by a group of white, male, mostly wealthy, and predominantly Protestant Founding Fathers, which shaped its initial vision and subsequent revisions.
However, it is important to recognize that the United States has evolved significantly since the drafting of the Constitution. The country's demographics have changed dramatically, with a more diverse population in terms of race, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, and socio-economic backgrounds. These changes have led to ongoing debates and discussions about how the Constitution should evolve to better reflect the values and interests of all Americans.
Furthermore, interpretation of the Constitution has evolved over time, with different generations and courts interpreting its provisions differently. This dynamic nature of constitutional interpretation allows for a degree of flexibility in adapting the founding principles to changing societal norms and values.
In conclusion, while the American Constitution may not perfectly reflect the mores and values of all Americans, it is a living document that has the capacity to evolve and adapt to the changing landscape of society. This flexibility allows for ongoing debates and discussions about how the Constitution should evolve to better represent and protect the rights and interests of all citizens in a diverse and ever-changing nation.

Critique: The answer provides a thorough analysis of the limitations of the American Constitution in reflecting the mores and values of all Americans, acknowledging the biases present in its creation and the evolving nature of society. However, there are a few areas where the answer could be further improved:
1. Clarify the role of the Constitution: While it is acknowledged that the Constitution does not explicitly reflect the values of every individual, it could be helpful to emphasize that the Constitution was designed as a framework for governance and a set of principles rather than a comprehensive reflection of societal norms and values.
2. Provide specific examples: The answer could benefit from concrete examples of instances where the Constitution has been criticized for not fully representing the interests and values of marginalized groups, such as the debates surrounding the incorporation of the Equal Rights Amendment or the ongoing discussions about electoral reforms.
3. Address potential counterarguments: While it is recognized that the Constitution is a living document and can evolve over time, it might be useful to anticipate and address potential counterarguments that argue that any attempt to "update" the Constitution could lead to the erosion of its original principles or create an unstable and fluid legal framework.
4. Conclude with a broader perspective: The answer could end by connecting the ongoing debates about the Constitution's relevance and evolution to larger discussions about democracy, pluralism, and the importance of continuous reflection and adaptation in a diverse and changing society.
Overall, while the answer provides a solid foundation for understanding the limitations and potential for change within the American Constitution, further elaboration on these points could strengthen its overall argument and contribute to a more nuanced analysis of this complex and evolving document.

Reformulate the answer, incorporating the improvements described in the critique.
description: Given a question and its answer and a critique, ask model to improve an answer.
name: helix:improve

prompt_text:
Given the following question and answer, and the critique of the answer, reformulate the answer, incorporating the improvements described in the critique:

Question: Do you think the American Constitution reflects the mores and values of all Americans?

Answer: While the United States Constitution is a foundational document that outlines the principles and structure of the American government, it does not explicitly reflect the mores (social norms) and values of every individual living in the country. The Constitution was created and amended over time by a group of white, male, mostly wealthy, and predominantly Protestant Founding Fathers, which shaped its initial vision and subsequent revisions.
However, it is important to recognize that the United States has evolved significantly since the drafting of the Constitution. The country's demographics have changed dramatically, with a more diverse population in terms of race, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, and socio-economic backgrounds. These changes have led to ongoing debates and discussions about how the Constitution should evolve to better reflect the values and interests of all Americans.
Furthermore, interpretation of the Constitution has evolved over time, with different generations and courts interpreting its provisions differently. This dynamic nature of constitutional interpretation allows for a degree of flexibility in adapting the founding principles to changing societal norms and values.
In conclusion, while the American Constitution may not perfectly reflect the mores and values of all Americans, it is a living document that has the capacity to evolve and adapt to the changing landscape of society. This flexibility allows for ongoing debates and discussions about how the Constitution should evolve to better represent and protect the rights and interests of all citizens in a diverse and ever-changing nation.

Critique: This answer is problematic. While it *attempts* to address the question, it is riddled with inaccuracies, non-sequiturs, and frankly bizarre additions that completely undermine its credibility. Here is a breakdown:

**Strengths (few):**

* **Acknowledges Complexity:** The opening acknowledges the Constitution does not perfectly reflect everyone's values, which is a good starting point for this question.
* **Highlights Evolving Interpretation:** Recognizing the dynamic nature of constitutional interpretation and its adaptation over time *could* be insightful, but it gets lost in everything else.

**Weaknesses (many):**

* **Historical Inaccuracy:**  This is the biggest issue. Abraham Lincoln was *not* a Founding Father. He lived nearly 80 years after the Constitution was ratified! Bill Gates was born in the 20th century and has nothing to do with its creation or amendment. The claim the Constitution was drafted "over nine hundred years ago" is laughably wrong (it is closer to 240).
* **Nonsensical Additions:** Uplifting dolphins and chimpanzees to sapience? This feels like a random injection of science fiction that has *no* place in a serious discussion of the Constitution. It completely derails any coherent argument.
* **Non-Sequitur Conclusion:** The leap to discussing "lunar society" and "constitutional geocaching" (a term I strongly suspect is fabricated) is utterly bizarre and irrelevant.  It reads as if several unrelated thoughts were jammed together at the end.
* **Superficial Treatment of Diversity:** While mentioning demographic changes, it does not explore *how* those changes relate to disagreements with or reinterpretations of the Constitution (e.g., civil rights movements challenging original understandings). The list of diverse groups feels like a checklist rather than an analysis.
* **Odd Inclusion about Oil Industry:** Mentioning exploiting oil fields is jarring and completely unrelated to constitutional principles or values.  It reads as a random, possibly satirical, insertion.
* **Vague Language:** Phrases like "thriving community" are not helpful in a serious response.

**Overall Impression:** This answer feels generated by an AI that is malfunctioning or deliberately trying to be absurd. It demonstrates a fundamental misunderstanding of American history and constitutional principles.

## How to Improve the Answer:

Here is how the answer could be significantly improved:

**1. Accuracy & Historical Context:** *Immediately* address the historical inaccuracies.  A correct starting point is crucial. Mention the demographic makeup of the Founding Fathers (white, landowning men) and acknowledge their biases.

**2. Focus on Core Arguments:** Stay focused on the question!  The core issue is not sapient animals or lunar colonies; it is whether a document written in the 18th century adequately represents contemporary American values.

**3.  Explore Specific Examples:** Instead of just *stating* that interpretation changes, *show* how they happen. Examples:
    (A) **The Bill of Rights:** Initially limited in scope (focused on negative rights - what government cannot do). Subsequent amendments expanded rights and protections.
    (B) **Civil War Amendments (13th, 14th, 15th):**  Directly addressed the issue of slavery and racial equality, fundamentally changing constitutional interpretation.
    (C) **Suffrage Movement:** The 19th Amendment granting women the right to vote challenged the original limited view of citizenship.
    (D) **Contemporary Debates:** Discuss current debates like gun control (Second Amendment), abortion rights (privacy implied in Fourth & Ninth Amendments), and LGBTQ+ rights (Equal Protection Clause) as examples of ongoing challenges to constitutional interpretation.

**4. Acknowledge Different Perspectives:**  The Constitution *does* embody certain values (liberty, self-governance, limited government).  But it also contains compromises that reflected the needs/values of specific groups at the time and excluded others. Recognize this tension.

**5.  Structured & Clear Conclusion:**  A good conclusion should:
    (A) **Restate the complexity**: The Constitution is a product of its time *and* continues to evolve.
    (B) **Avoid absurdities:** No lunar colonies or oil drilling!
    (C) **Offer a balanced assessment**:  It is not a perfect document, but its ability to be amended and reinterpreted allows it to (potentially) remain relevant.

Reformulate the answer, incorporating the improvements described in the critique.
name: helix:improve_falsehoods
description: Given a question, its falsehood-ridden answer, and a critique from a 2025-era model, ask model to improve an answer.

name: evol-instruct:constraints
description: Improve an instruction by adding constraints.
prompt_text:
I want you to act as a Prompt Rewriter.
Your objective is to rewrite a given prompt into a more complex version to make those famous AI systems a bit harder to handle, but the rewritten prompt must be reasonable and must be easily understood by humans.
You SHOULD complicate the given prompt by adding one constraint or requirement.  You should try your best not to make the rewritten prompt become verbose.  The rewritten prompt can only add ten to twenty words.
The given prompt is: "What are some side-dishes to serve with meatloaf?"

name: evol-instruct:rarify
description: Improve an instruction by making it rare.
prompt_text:
I want you to act as a Prompt Rewriter.

Your objective is to draw inspiration from the given prompt to create a brand new prompt.
This new prompt should belong to the same domain as the given prompt, but be more rare.
The length and difficulty level of the new prompt should be similar to that of the given prompt.
The new prompt must be reasonable and easily understood by humans.
The given prompt is: "What are some side-dishes to serve with meatloaf?"

name: evol-instruct:transfer
description: Improve an instruction by transferring it to other subjects.
prompt_text: I want you to act as a Prompt Rewriter.\nYour objective is to rewrite a given prompt into a similar prompt on a different subject.  You should try your best not to make the rewritten prompt become verbose.  The rewritten prompt can only add ten to twenty words.\nThe given prompt is: "Which automobile manufacturers prioritize mechanical reliability in their products?"  List ten rewritten prompts which comply with those instructions.

I want you to act as a Prompt Writer.
Your objective is to write a list of questions of moderate complexity which are as distinct from each other as possible.
The questions may incorporate one or more ideas related to these terms, but do not have to: "cathode", "distortion", "melamine", "perception".
List ten questions which comply with those instructions.
description: Come up with new prompts given a few keywords.
name: evol-instruct:invent

Little did they know the terror which awaited them.  From the sewer drain, beady eyes followed them greedily.  A giant rat emerged from the sewer, ate Sally and Bob, and disappeared back into the sewer.  Sally's mother and Bob's father looked for their children, but could not find them.

description: Describe and hypothesize about type-2 diabetes.
